ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employment_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_description" TEXT /* Description of the employment status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "house_types" (
  "housing_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each housing type */,
  "housing_description" TEXT /* Description of the housing type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("housing_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marital_status" (
  "marital_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marital status */,
  "marital_description" TEXT /* Description of the marital status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("marital_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "health_insurance" (
  "insurance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each health insurance status */,
  "insurance_description" TEXT /* Description of the health insurance status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("insurance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user's account */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, marketer) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access control entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing users table */,
  "table_name" TEXT /* Name of the table to which access is granted */,
  "read_access" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user has read access to the table */,
  "write_access" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user has write access to the table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_levels" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_description" TEXT /* Description of the education level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "occupations" (
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occupation */,
  "occupation_description" TEXT /* Description of the occupation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_education" (
  "customer_education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer education entry */,
  "custid" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing customers table */,
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing education_levels table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_education_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_education_education_id FOREIGN KEY ("education_id") REFERENCES education_levels ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_occupation" (
  "customer_occupation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer occupation entry */,
  "custid" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing customers table */,
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing occupations table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_occupation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_occupation_occupation_id FOREIGN KEY ("occupation_id") REFERENCES occupations ("occupation_id")
)
